skill.name: example-skill
skill.description: Reference guidance for creating Claude Code plugin skills, including structure, frontmatter, descriptions, and content practices.
skill.summary: Create clear Claude Code skill templates and descriptions.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only markdown examples and descriptive text. No executable code, shell invocation, cryptographic implementation, system probing, network access, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur inside markdown code fences that show folder layouts and YAML text. They do not execute Ruby backticks or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown fence delimiters around examples. No executable Ruby, shell syntax, or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections match ordinary documentation words such as description and descriptions. SKILL.md contains no cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, cipher configuration, or secret handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious text is prose about skill descriptions. There is no code path where DES, MD5, SHA-1, or another weak algorithm is used.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detection points to guidance about avoiding overlapping trigger conditions. No command, file read, host inspection, or environment enumeration appears on this line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is normal documentation text. It contains no system discovery behavior or instruction to gather local machine details.

content.value_statement: Writing a skill from scratch can be unclear for new plugin authors. This skill gives a compact reference for structure, frontmatter, descriptions, and skill content.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md file for a plugin skill.
Shows optional supporting folders for references, examples, and scripts.
Lists supported frontmatter fields for a skill definition.
Guides authors on writing trigger descriptions.
Summarizes content guidelines for focused and actionable skills.
Provides best practices for avoiding trigger overlap.
limitations: Does not include automated validation or linting tools.
Does not provide complete marketplace packaging instructions.
Does not include advanced multi-file skill examples.
Does not execute helper scripts or inspect a repository.

use_cases.title: Draft a First Skill
use_cases.description: Use the template to understand the minimum files and fields required for a new skill.

use_cases.title: Review Skill Descriptions
use_cases.description: Compare a skill description against trigger guidance before publishing it.

use_cases.title: Teach Skill Structure
use_cases.description: Use the reference as a short explanation of how Claude Code skills are organized.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the basic structure of a Claude Code skill using the example skill format.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a New Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a focused Claude Code skill for [domain], including trigger phrases and required files.

prompt_templates.title: Improve My Description
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill description and suggest clearer trigger wording: [paste description].

prompt_templates.title: Audit Skill Scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this skill outline for scope, activation clarity, overlap risks, and missing reference materials: [paste outline].

output_examples.input: Show me the expected files for a simple skill.
output_examples.output: The response explains that SKILL.md is required and describes optional folders for references, examples, and scripts.

output_examples.input: Help me write a description for a testing skill.
output_examples.output: The response proposes clear trigger phrases, relevant keywords, and a concise description focused on testing workflows.

output_examples.input: Review my skill outline for publishing readiness.
output_examples.output: The response identifies scope issues, unclear activation conditions, and places where supporting references would help.

best_practices: Keep each skill focused on one clear domain.
Use specific trigger phrases and keywords in descriptions.
Add reference materials when guidance becomes too long for SKILL.md.
anti_patterns: Do not write vague descriptions that could activate too often.
Do not duplicate another skill's trigger conditions.
Do not place complex domain knowledge only in the main skill file.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps authors understand the structure and writing patterns for Claude Code plugin skills.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete plugins?
faq.answer: No. It provides skill guidance, not full plugin scaffolding or packaging.

faq.question: What file is required for a skill?
faq.answer: The example states that SKILL.md is the main required skill definition file.

faq.question: Can it help with trigger descriptions?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains that descriptions should include phrases, keywords, and clear topic areas.

faq.question: Does it run scripts?
faq.answer: No. It only documents that scripts may be optional supporting files.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation-only content and no confirmed dangerous behavior.
